name: Unleash MCP Server (Official)
what_it_does: Official Unleash MCP server enabling AI agents to interact with Unleash's feature management platform — querying feature flag states, creating and updating toggles, managing strategies and rollout rules, and integrating Unleash's feature flag system into agent-driven release management and experimentation workflows.
use_cases: Querying feature flag states from release management agents
Creating and updating feature toggles from deployment automation agents
Managing gradual rollout percentages from progressive delivery agents
Disabling features for incident response from on-call agents
Auditing feature flag configurations from compliance agents
Coordinating feature releases across environments from CI/CD agents
not_for: Teams using LaunchDarkly, Split.io, or Statsig for feature management
A/B testing at statistical significance scale (use dedicated experimentation platforms)
best_when: An agent needs to query or manage Unleash feature flags — especially for automated release management, incident response, or progressive delivery.
avoid_when: You're using LaunchDarkly, Split.io, or another feature flag platform.

auth.notes: Unleash admin API tokens with role-based access (Admin, Editor, Viewer). Separate client tokens for SDK feature evaluation. Environment-specific tokens supported.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Open source: fully free, self-hosted; Cloud: free up to 5 users
pricing.notes: Unleash Open Source is free and self-hostable. Cloud offering has free tier. Enterprise adds SSO, advanced audit logs, and dedicated support. MCP server is official open source.

performance.rate_limits: Self-hosted: configurable; Cloud: rate limits by plan

known_agent_gotchas: Admin API token vs Client SDK token are different — use Admin token for management operations
Unleash projects contain feature flags — must specify correct project context
Environment-specific flag states — production vs staging have separate configurations
Strategy evaluation happens client-side — MCP server manages configuration, not evaluation
Self-hosted vs Cloud Unleash have same API but different base URLs
Variants and custom strategies require understanding Unleash data model
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS enforced for Cloud; TLS configurable for self-hosted. Role-based admin tokens. SOC 2, GDPR. Official Unleash MCP server. Self-hosted security is operator responsibility.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced (Cloud). Role-based admin tokens. SOC 2, GDPR. Official Unleash MCP server. Self-hosted TLS is operator responsibility.
